dependencies.description: IS-IS runs directly over Layer 2 (SAP 0xFE) and does not require IP connectivity for neighbor formation. L2 reachability is sufficient.
dependencies.name: Layer 2 connectivity

dependencies.description: A Network Entity Title (NET) must be configured under the IS-IS process. The NET encodes the area address and system ID (e.g., 49.0001.1921.6800.1001.00).
dependencies.name: NET address configured

dependencies.description: IS-IS must be explicitly enabled on each participating interface (NX-OS/IOS-XR: under router isis; IOS-XE: 'ip router isis' under interface).
dependencies.name: IS-IS enabled on interfaces

dependencies.description: Neighbors must share at least one common level (L1 or L2). L1 neighbors must also share at least one area address.
dependencies.name: Matching level/area configuration

failure_modes.description: A neighbor remains in INITIALIZING state indefinitely. IIHs are being received from the neighbor, but the neighbor does not include our system ID in its IIH, indicating one-way communication or a configuration mismatch preventing the adjacency from completing the three-way handshake.

resolutions.action: Verify area addresses match: 'show isis protocol' on both routers
Verify area addresses match: 'show isis protocol' on both routers
Verify area addresses match: 'show isis protocol' on both routers
Check level configuration: ensure both sides share at least one common level (L1, L2, or L1L2)
Check level configuration: ensure both sides share at least one common level (L1, L2, or L1L2)
Check level configuration: ensure both sides share at least one common level (L1, L2, or L1L2)
Verify authentication: compare key-chain and auth mode on both ends
Verify authentication: compare key-chain and auth mode on both ends
Verify authentication: compare key-chain and auth mode on both ends
Check MTU: IS-IS runs directly on L2 and PDUs are not IP-fragmented — ensure MTU matches
Check MTU: IS-IS runs directly on L2 and PDUs are not IP-fragmented — ensure MTU matches
Check MTU: IS-IS runs directly on L2 and PDUs are not IP-fragmented — ensure MTU matches
Verify system IDs are unique across the IS-IS domain
Verify system IDs are unique across the IS-IS domain
Verify system IDs are unique across the IS-IS domain

root_causes.cause: Level mismatch (one side L1-only, other L2-only)
MTU issue — IS-IS PDUs can be up to interface MTU and are not fragmented
System ID conflict (duplicate system IDs in the domain)

symptoms: show isis neighbors shows neighbor in INIT, not UP
Adjacency does not progress beyond INITIALIZING state

failure_modes.description: Adjacency is UP but routes are missing from the routing table. The LSDB is incomplete or not converging, often due to LSP fragmentation issues, CSNP/PSNP drops, or metric style incompatibility between routers.

resolutions.action: Verify MTU consistency: IS-IS LSPs can be up to the interface MTU; ensure all links in the area have matching MTU
Verify MTU consistency: IS-IS LSPs can be up to the interface MTU; ensure all links in the area have matching MTU
Verify MTU consistency: IS-IS LSPs can be up to the interface MTU; ensure all links in the area have matching MTU
Check CSNP/PSNP counters: 'show isis neighbors detail' — look for sent/received imbalance
Check CSNP/PSNP counters: 'show isis neighbors detail' — look for sent/received imbalance
Check CSNP/PSNP counters: 'show isis neighbors detail' — look for sent/received imbalance
Standardize metric style: configure 'metric-style wide' across the domain
Standardize metric style: configure 'metric-style wide' across the domain
Standardize metric style: configure 'metric-style wide' across the domain
Check for LSP overload bit: 'show isis database detail' — look for ATT/OL flags
Check for LSP overload bit: 'show isis database detail' — look for ATT/OL flags
Check for LSP overload bit: 'show isis database detail' — look for ATT/OL flags

root_causes.cause: MTU preventing large LSPs from being received (LSPs are not fragmented at IP level)
CSNP or PSNP being dropped (L2 filtering or queuing issue)
Metric style mismatch — narrow metrics (max 63) vs wide metrics (max 16777215)

symptoms: Routes missing despite adjacency showing UP
show isis database shows fewer LSPs than expected
CSNP/PSNP counters incrementing but LSDB incomplete

failure_modes.description: L1 routers cannot see L2 routes, unexpected route leaking occurs, or adjacency fails to form because of incompatible level or area configurations between neighbors.

resolutions.action: Verify level configuration: 'show isis protocol' — ensure neighbors share a common level
Verify level configuration: 'show isis protocol' — ensure neighbors share a common level
Verify level configuration: 'show isis protocol' — ensure neighbors share a common level
Configure route leaking if L1 routes need to reach L2 or vice versa
Configure route leaking if L1 routes need to reach L2 or vice versa
Configure route leaking if L1 routes need to reach L2 or vice versa
Check area addresses: all routers in the same L1 area must share at least one area address
Check area addresses: all routers in the same L1 area must share at least one area address
Check area addresses: all routers in the same L1 area must share at least one area address
Verify interface-level circuit type: 'show isis interface' — ensure correct level assignment
Verify interface-level circuit type: 'show isis interface' — ensure correct level assignment
Verify interface-level circuit type: 'show isis interface' — ensure correct level assignment

root_causes.cause: Circuit-type mismatch (one side L1, other L2 with no common level)
NSEL or area address mismatch between neighbors

symptoms: L1 router cannot see L2 routes or vice versa
Adjacency fails to form despite IIHs being exchanged

failure_modes.description: Suboptimal routing or unexpected path selection caused by inconsistent metric styles within the IS-IS domain. Narrow metrics cap at 63 per hop (1023 total path), while wide metrics allow up to 16,777,215 — mixing them causes unpredictable behavior.

resolutions.action: Standardize on 'metric-style wide' across all routers in the domain
Standardize on 'metric-style wide' across all routers in the domain
Standardize on 'metric-style wide' across all routers in the domain
Verify interface metrics: 'show isis interface' — check metric values
Verify interface metrics: 'show isis interface' — check metric values
Verify interface metrics: 'show isis interface' — check metric values
Use 'metric-style transition' temporarily during migration from narrow to wide
Use 'metric-style transition' temporarily during migration from narrow to wide
Use 'metric-style transition' temporarily during migration from narrow to wide
Set explicit interface metrics to ensure consistent path costs
Set explicit interface metrics to ensure consistent path costs
Set explicit interface metrics to ensure consistent path costs

root_causes.cause: Mixed narrow and wide metric configurations in the domain
Default interface metric not explicitly set (defaults may differ by OS)
Legacy routers still using narrow metrics after migration
metric-style transition mode masking the mismatch

symptoms: Suboptimal routing — traffic takes unexpected paths
Metrics capped at 63 per hop in show isis database
Segment routing or TE not functioning (requires wide metrics)

messages.name: LSP (Link State PDU)
messages.when_sent: On topology change or periodic refresh

messages.description: Synchronizes LSDBs — lists all LSPs known
messages.name: CSNP (Complete Sequence Number PDU)
messages.when_sent: Periodically by DIS on LAN, at startup on P2P

messages.description: Requests missing LSPs or acknowledges received LSPs
messages.name: PSNP (Partial Sequence Number PDU)
messages.when_sent: When CSNP reveals missing or newer LSP in LSDB

purpose: Intermediate System to Intermediate System — link-state IGP supporting IPv4, IPv6, and segment routing

states.description: No IS-IS Hello (IIH) received from neighbor. Initial state for all adjacencies. The local system has no knowledge of the remote IS on this circuit.
states.name: DOWN

states.description: An IIH has been received from the neighbor, but our own SNPA or system ID has not yet been seen in the neighbor's IIH. Communication is one-way at this point. Being stuck in INITIALIZING indicates a problem with bidirectional communication.
states.name: INITIALIZING

states.description: Bidirectional adjacency established — own system ID has been seen in the neighbor's IIH (three-way handshake passed). LSP exchange is in progress or complete and the LSDB is synchronizing.
states.name: UP

timers.name: hello-interval
timers.tuning_advice: Must match all neighbors on a segment for LAN circuits. Lower values speed detection but increase overhead; consider BFD for sub-second detection instead.

timers.name: hello-multiplier
timers.tuning_advice: Increasing the multiplier improves tolerance for missed Hellos but slows failure detection.

timers.description: Complete Sequence Number PDU interval (LAN DIS only)
timers.name: CSNP interval
timers.on_expiry: DIS sends CSNP to synchronize LSDBs
timers.tuning_advice: Rarely needs tuning. The DIS sends CSNPs periodically to keep LSDBs synchronized on multi-access segments.

timers.name: LSP refresh
timers.on_expiry: LSP re-originated with incremented sequence number
timers.tuning_advice: Must be less than LSP max-age to prevent premature purge. Default is appropriate for most networks.

timers.name: LSP max-age
timers.tuning_advice: Increase in large or unstable networks to avoid premature purges. Must be greater than LSP refresh interval.

timers.name: SPF delay
timers.tuning_advice: Use aggressive SPF timers (e.g., 50ms/200ms/5000ms) for sub-second convergence. The three values represent initial-delay / hold-time / max-wait.

transitions.trigger: Own system ID seen in neighbor's IIH (bidirectional check passed)
Hold timer expired or interface down

transitions.failure_if_stuck: One-way communication — possible L2 filtering, MTU issue, or area/level mismatch

transport: ISO CLNP — directly over Layer 2 (SAP 0xFE), no IP required

notes.note: IS-IS uses 'router isis' with NET configuration. Supports both narrow and wide metrics. 'metric-style wide' recommended for modern deployments and required for SR.
IS-IS is the preferred IGP on IOS-XR platforms. Configured under 'router isis <tag>' with interface hierarchy. Wide metrics and SR support built-in. Supports TI-LFA and microloop avoidance.
IOS-XR IS-IS supports single-topology and multi-topology modes for IPv4/IPv6. Multi-topology required when IPv4 and IPv6 topologies differ.
IS-IS requires 'feature isis'. Uses wide metrics by default. Configure NET under 'router isis' and enable per-interface. Supports segment routing TI-LFA.
